pattern: Generate palindromes in increasing order by mirroring a growing root, skipping the even-digit-length band entirely since those palindromes are all divisible by eleven, and return the first that is also prime.
hints: Searching every integer is wasteful; it is far cheaper to enumerate palindromes directly and test each for primality.
Any palindrome with an even number of digits is a multiple of eleven, so apart from eleven itself you can skip that whole range of lengths.

Build palindromes by taking a root, mirroring it (with and without a shared middle digit) to form candidates in ascending order, and return the first candidate at least n that passes a primality test.

edge_inputs.description: first example: smallest prime palindrome at least six is seven
second example: skip the even-digit band to reach eleven
third example: jump past all two-digit palindromes to one hundred one
value one yields the smallest prime palindrome two
eleven is itself a prime palindrome
just above eleven forces a three-digit answer
a three-digit start lands on a three-digit prime palindrome
four-digit start jumps the whole even band to a five-digit answer

stress.description: random starting values kept modest so the candidate scan stays quick
stress.note: single scalar; capped so the brute oracle finishes fast
